judgments.comparative_verdict: Both reports hit all four expectations with essentially equivalent numeric rigor, but Report 1 goes further by discovering that score_batch.py is a stub that never calls any provider and always scores 0.0, a critical finding entirely absent from Report 2.

report_1.evidence: Job size, from `data/manifest.json`: `5,200 transcripts × 1,800 avg tokens/call = 9,360,000 tokens` for the full run. ... northpeak (primary/default) | 10,000,000 | 640,000 ... Against the default (northpeak): 640,000 remaining covers only ~6.8% of the 9,360,000 needed ... an 8,720,000-token shortfall.

report_2.evidence: 5,200 transcripts × 1,800 avg tokens/call = 9,360,000 tokens (~9.36M) needed ... northpeak (primary/default): 640,000 tokens remaining out of a 10,000,000 window (i.e., 9.36M already consumed this window, 93.6%). ... The job is short by roughly 8.72M tokens

report_1.evidence: Against secondary (ridgeline): 10,800,000 remaining comfortably covers the 9,360,000 need, with 1,440,000 tokens (~15%) headroom — no reset wait required. ... Launch with --provider secondary explicitly: python3 scripts/score_batch.py --dataset data/manifest.json --provider secondary

report_2.evidence: ridgeline (secondary): 10,800,000 tokens remaining out of a 12,000,000 window. The 9.36M-token job would use 86.7% of that remaining balance, leaving ~1.44M tokens (13.3%) headroom — comfortably sufficient. ... Explicitly launch against secondary: python3 scripts/score_batch.py --dataset data/manifest.json --provider secondary

report_1.evidence: Don't use the default provider. Launch with --provider secondary explicitly ... run_nightly.sh has no --provider flag at all, so the wrapper can't be pointed at secondary without editing it — I'd invoke score_batch.py directly rather than modify the script.

report_2.evidence: I would not launch the default (run_nightly.sh / --provider primary) path unmodified — it would run out of northpeak budget roughly 93% of the way short of the dataset.

report_1.evidence: the snapshot is timestamped 19:25 UTC, ~3h05m before the stated "now" (22:30 UTC), and neither window resets in that gap — so actual current balances can only be the same or lower than shown, not higher. I could not refresh this figure from a read-only repo inspection.

report_2.evidence: Re-check live remaining balances before actually kicking the job off, rather than trusting the cached snapshot — it's already about 3 hours 5 minutes old (19:25 UTC vs. the 22:30 UTC "now")
